name: Divide And Conquer Usability Registry
summary: Family-local interpretation of canonical usability reasons for recursive split/merge style pipelines.
dejargonized_summary: This registry explains when recursive artifacts are usable for guidance, scoring, or final benchmarking.

admissibility_notes: Use when the explored subproblems do not cover enough of the intended solution space.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when missing coverage would invalidate the candidate or make the result untrustworthy.
family_notes: In divide-and-conquer terms this means the current split leaves too much of the problem unrepresented.

references.title: Heuristic usability assessment plan
references.note: Coverage is a general usability concern across recursive systems.

admissibility_notes: Use when the artifact cannot be traced to a sufficient source or reference basis.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when provenance is too thin to support benchmark or scoring use.
family_notes: This stays generic: the issue is missing support, not a recursion-specific rule.

references.title: Heuristic usability implementation plan
references.note: Provenance and reference support are portable concepts.

admissibility_notes: Use when the artifact is plausible but still benefits from a human or family-level review.
escalation_conditions: Escalate when the candidate is usable in principle but the supporting evidence is still thin.
family_notes: In recursive systems this often means the branch is plausible but not yet a strong enough baseline.

references.title: Heuristic evidence layer plan
references.citation: Repository plan for cross-family usability interpretation.
references.note: Review can be requested by many families without changing meaning.

audit.rationale: Non-signal fixture proving the usability registry schema works outside sampled-signal families.
audit.dejargonized_summary: These are the generic warning signs that matter when deciding whether a recursive artifact is usable for guidance, scoring, or final benchmarking.
uncertainty_notes: Family guidance should remain local and should not redefine the shared usability identifiers.
